title: TCGM Engineer (Total Chemical & Gas Management)
description:
About our client: Our client is an EPC company specializing in the chemical, petrochemical, and high-tech industries.

About the role: 

  • Support the TCGM Manager in the overall management of TCGM (Total Chemical & Gas Management) operations at client sites.
  • Attend customer meetings, provide technical updates, and submit daily operation reports.
  • Lead and supervise a team of 6 technicians to ensure smooth operations and strict compliance with safety regulations.
  • Plan and execute daily TCGM operations and preventive maintenance for chemical and gas distribution systems within semiconductor facilities.
  • Conduct inspections to ensure chemical, slurry, and gas system integrity in accordance with Standard Operating Procedures (SOP).
  • Troubleshoot equipment and system issues, including pumps, valves, filters, gas cabinets, valve manifold boxes (VMB), and valve manifold panels (VMP).
  • Manage on-site spare parts inventory and personal protective equipment (PPE).
  • Monitor and analyze system performance, quality trends, and reliability data to support continuous improvement.
  • Coordinate with customers to provide technical support and ensure sufficient chemical and gas materials are available within permitted usage limits.
  • Train and coach team members, and coordinate with safety officers on required certifications and training courses.
  • Maintain high housekeeping standards and 5S practices in all TCGM-managed areas.
  • Perform any other related duties as assigned.

About you:

  • Possess a Diploma in Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, E&I Engineering, or equivalent working experience in TCGM, TCM, or TGM-related operations or maintenance.
  • Proven supervisory skills in leading and managing a team, with strong interpersonal and communication skills.
  • Hands-on experience in chemical, slurry, or gas system operations and/or troubleshooting is an advantage.
  • Basic chemical knowledge and experience in chemical or gas operations, including understanding of P&ID and system design.
  • Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving skills. Experience in maintenance of pumps, valves, filters, gas cabinets, VMB, VMP, and related equipment.
  • Willingness to work overtime when required.
  • Good adaptability and flexibility.
  • Work location is in the East of Singapore; candidates must be willing to work on-site.


Other info:

  • Work location: Pasir Ris
  • Work hours: Monday - Friday (8:30am - 5.30pm)

*Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.*


Employment Agency: Kingsforce Management Services Pte. Ltd.

EA Personnel: Kimberly Wong
